_comment: Work template (book/biography/novel) — copy into content/works/<work-slug>.json. The file name should equal the id (validator warns on mismatch). Keys starting with _ are authoring comments; the validator strips them.
_id: kebab-case ascii slug, unique across ALL entity types, e.g. "ben-gurion-biography-teveth".
_workType: id from content/taxonomies/work-types.json (currently: biography | autobiography | historical-novel). Adding a work type is a content change, not a code change.
_description: 1-3 Hebrew sentences: what the work is and why it matters.

description.he: תיאור קצר של היצירה, משפט אחד עד שלושה.

_authorPersonIds: Authors who are themselves timeline people (ids from content/people). A work needs authorPersonIds and/or authorName.
_authorName: Plain Hebrew display name when the author is not a timeline person.
_subjectPersonIds: Who the work is about (ids from content/people). For an autobiography: same person as the author.
_subjectEventIds: Events the work describes (ids from content/events).
_publicationDate: When the work was published — stored for future views, NOT the timeline position (decision D7).
_coveredPeriod: The historical period the work DESCRIBES — the timeline position derives from this (decision D7). Same date format as everywhere: "YYYY" | "YYYY-MM" | "YYYY-MM-DD".
_importance: Integer 1-100 per the docs/05 rubric: most works sit at 20-39 (contextual, year-level zoom); important/canonical works 40-69; 1-19 niche works (month-level zoom).
_regionIds: ids from content/taxonomies/regions.json the work is set in.
_links: Optional related reading / catalog links. NOT the citation — use "sources". Placeholder URLs ("...", example.com) are rejected.
_sources: REQUIRED — at least one citation (e.g. the publisher, a library catalogue, or a review). Add "url" only when it is a real, stable page. "publisher"/"kind" optional (archive|library|museum|encyclopedia|reference|academic|government|book|press|website).
